content.full_text:
Fern Polrrrcal PRAcrrcps C oMMrssroN
P.O. Box 807 o 428 J Street o Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-s660 . Fax (916) 322-0886
January 23,2002
Sharee Washer
P.O. Box 3050
California Valley, CA 93453
Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-01-304
Dear Ms. Washer:
This is in response to your request for advice under the conflict-of-interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 Because your question does not
concern a specific governmental decision, we are teatingyour request as one for
informal assistance.2
QUESTTON
As the director of a community services district, would your position as a
volunteer firefighter preclude you from participating in a decision of the district to
upgrade a fire station?
CONCLUSION
No. Your position as a volunteer firefighter is not an economic interest that
would require you to abstain from participating in this decision.
FACTS
You are a director on the California Valley Community Services District.
Additionally, you are a volunteer firefighter for the California Department of Forestry
and earn $12 for every call to which you respond. The district will be voting soon to
1 Government Code sections 81000 - glOT4. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
1 8 1 0 9 - 1 8 997, of the California Code of Regulations.
2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or
formal written advice. (Section 83 1 14; Reg. 18329(c)(3), copy enclosed.)
-"rq-,tr


Advice Letter I-01-304
Page2
upgrade a fire station that sits on district property. You are concerned that your volunteer
firefighter position precludes you from participating in this decision.
ANALYSIS
The Political Reform Act prohibits public officials from making, participating or
using their position to influence a governmental decision that may have a material
financial effect on the official, a member of his or her immediate family, or any economic
interest. (Sections 87100, 87103.)
In addition to investments, real property, and gifts, an economic interest includes
any source of income aggregating five hundred dollars ($500) or more in value provided,
promised or received by the public official within 12 months prior to the time when a
decision is made. (Section 87103(c).)
"Income" is defined as "a payment received, including but not limited to any
salary, wage, advance, dividend, interest, rent, proceeds from any sale, gift, including any
gift of food or beverage, loan, forgiveness or payment of indebtedness received by the
frler ;' (Section 82030(a).)
"Income" does not include any "[s]alary and reimbursement for expenses or per
diem received from a state, local, or federal government agency...." (Section
8203 0(b)(2) and Regulation 18232.)
Even though your volunteer position with the Department of Forestry does bring a
salary of $12 for each call, that payment is a salary from a governmental source and is not
an economic interest that would require your disqualification. You may participate in the
district's decision concerning the fire station upgrade.
I trust this answers your question sufficiently. If you have additional questions,
please feel free to call me at (866) 275-3772.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
Counsel
u\.D)[
By: Jeanette E. Turvill
Political Reform Consultant
Technical Assistance Division
IET/jt
Enclosure

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: As the director of a community services district, would your position as a
volunteer firefighter preclude you from participating in a decision of the district to
upgrade a fire station?

CONCLUSION: No. Your position as a volunteer firefighter is not an economic interest that
would require you to abstain from participating in this decision.
